input.headline: Bok coach Rassie: 'I would be lying if I said we don't want to tick the Ireland box'

raw.headline: Bok coach Rassie: 'I would be lying if I said we don't want to tick the Ireland box'

winner: Rassie says he's not a liar, just a *bok*-er—so he'll definitely tick the Ireland box... unless they play on a Sunday, then he's got church plans.

candidates.text: I'd be lying if I said our team wouldn't celebrate scoring against Ireland—especially after they spent the whole match pretending to be a defensive line.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.2, "reason": "Surprising twist on the headline with a specific sports reference, good pun, and natural flow."}

candidates.text: Rassie says he's not a liar, just a *bok*-er—so he'll definitely tick the Ireland box... unless they play on a Sunday, then he's got church plans.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, uses wordplay on 'bok' and 'box,' and adds a specific, surprising twist with the church plans."}

candidates.text: Why did the South African rugby coach bring a bingo card to his match against Ireland? He wanted to make sure he didn’t miss any of their "tick boxes.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat related to the headline but lacks clarity and punch. The reference to 'tick boxes' is a bit forced and doesn't fully align with the original quote's context."}

candidates.text: Rassie: "I'd love to tick the Ireland box, but our team's already in a state of perpetual check—every match is a new crossword.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is clever with a play on 'tick the Ireland box' and 'perpetual check,' but the crossword analogy feels slightly forced and could be more concise."}

candidates.text: Rassie’s team was on fire, but he still couldn’t resist a cheeky dig at Ireland—then his coach walked in and said, 'Actually, we *definitely* want to beat them.'
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat humorous with the twist involving the coach walking in, but it's a bit generic and lacks specificity. The reference to 'tick the Ireland box' isn't fully utilized, and the humor could be sharper."}
